For remediation, we need to modify /etc/sysctl.d/ or /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d, etc. In practice, we can create /etc/sysctl.d/99-kexec.conf file containing "kernel.kexec_load_disabled=1". But we must not duplicate, should use sed to modify existing values if present.
